doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’m glad to be here. So, I’ve been having this constant pain in my right knee for about two weeks now. It’s, like, right below the kneecap and it’s been really achy, you know? There’s also some swelling and it feels warm and red. I’m kinda worried about it.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your knee?
patient_turn: Uh, I first noticed it about two weeks ago. It just started out of nowhere, honestly. I mean, I didn’t have any trauma or anything, so it was really surprising.

doctor_turn: Has the pain changed at all since it first started?
patient_turn: Um, yeah, it’s pretty much been the same, I think. It’s mostly achy, but sometimes it gets sharp, you know? It’s rated about a 6 out of 10 in severity, and it definitely gets worse when I kneel or try to bend my knee. But, um, keeping it straight helps a bit.

doctor_turn: Where exactly do you feel the pain in your knee?
patient_turn: It's, um, right below the kneecap, you know? Like, in that area, and it’s really tender to touch. I can feel the swelling there too, which is kinda concerning.

doctor_turn: Does the pain ever spread to other areas, like your thigh or calf?
patient_turn: No, um, it doesn’t really spread anywhere else. It’s just localized to that spot below the kneecap. I haven’t felt any pain in my thigh or calf, so that’s a relief, I guess.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your knee?

doctor_turn: Have you had any recent injuries or trauma to your knee?
